pinned_comment.body:
Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램 MV3을 사용하시고 숲 광고 차단이 안되는 분들께:
해당 이슈는 최근 변경 사항 ( https://github.com/List-KR/List-KR/commit/29e530df948a1054fba818a1917992c80c3b2d04 )이 Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3에 아직 적용되지 않아서 발생한 현상입니다.
따라서, Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3를 사용하시는 분들은 아래 방법을 따라주시기 바랍니다:
 1. 각 브라우저의 웹 확장 프로그램 관리 페이지에 들어갑니다. (예: `chrome://extensions/`)
 2. Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램의 디테일 버튼을 선택합니다.
 3. 사용자 스크립트 권한을 부여합니다.
 4. 브라우저 툴바에 있는 Adguard 브라우저 확장 프로그램을 클릭하고 필터 리스트 업데이트 버튼을 누릅니다.
 5. 숲 웹 페이지를 새로고침합니다.
 6. 그럼에도 해결되지 않으면 다음 정식 Adguard 브라우저 확장 MV3의 릴리즈 시기까지 기다리시고 확장 프로그램 자체를 업데이트하셔야 합니다.

이러한 번거러움은 Google의 정책에서 비롯되었습니다.

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://overe-shop.com/collections](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fovere-shop.com%2Fcollections) ### Comment > At some Websites, even the product images also gets banned. But I couldn't find a solution to show the image again without technically allowing the domain itself. As the site has lots of annoying ADs, I can't just allow the whole website domain to escape the DNS filter. Instead, I want them to be firm and steady, letting the product images to show up. Is there any solution with this? ``` Username: @rezz012 ``` ### Screenshots

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Anti Adblock Script) [https://edmontonjournal.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fedmontonjournal.com%2F) ### Comment > The Edmonton Journal news website along with all the Post Media (news conglomerate websites, like the National Post) are doing this. They are blocking access while an ad blocker is running. This also happens in Google News. I even pay for a subscription to them! ### Screenshots

labels.description: Elements that annoy users

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [NSFW] [https://b-short.link/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fb-short.link%2F) ### Comment > # Report: Suspicious Use of b-short.link on Social Media > > ## Summary > > I’m writing to report some suspicious activity involving the URL shortening service **b-short.link**. > > Recently, I have noticed many posts on social media platforms like **X (formerly Twitter)** and **Threads** that try to trick people into clicking links by using bait phrases such as: > > * "Click here to see nudes" > * "Click here to see celebrity nudes" > > These phrases are clearly designed to attract clicks through misleading or deceptive claims. > > ## Behavior Observed > > When users click these links, they are almost never taken to the content they were promised. Instead, they are typically redirected to: > > * sexual dating applications > * external sign-up pages > * unrelated affiliate landing pages > > This appears to be a form of misleading clickbait and, in many cases, fraudulent advertising. > > ## Additional Concerns > > Some of these posts use the names of real celebrities or imply the existence of leaked private images. This raises serious concerns related to: > > * defamation > * privacy violations > * impersonation-style traffic manipulation > > While **b-short.link** may have legitimate uses, it is currently being widely used for deceptive redirection campaigns on social media platforms. > > ## Evidence (Search Results Showing Multiple Cases) > > Examples of related posts can be observed here: > > * [https://x.com/search?q=lang%3Aja%20b-short.link&src=typed_query&f=top](https://x.com/search?q=lang%3Aja%20b-short.link&src=typed_query&f=top) > * [https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.threads.com%2F+b-short.link](https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.threads.com%2F+b-short.link) > > ## Impact > > These posts appear frequently across X and Threads and may expose users to: > > * deceptive advertising > * unwanted subscriptions > * scam-style redirects > * privacy risks > > ## Request > > I hope you can investigate this activity and consider whether **b-short.link** should be reviewed for inclusion in filtering lists or abuse monitoring systems. > > Thank you for your time and for helping maintain a safer browsing environment. ### [NSFW] Screenshots [Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/i23vdxmd5a80k40owsgscw8o4w4kcgcqt2edlhpyqo.png?nc=1) ### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Windows v7.22.7 System version: | `Windows11,25H2` Browser: | Chrome AdGuard driver: | TDI Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Social Widgets:**
AdGuard Social Media filter

AdGuard Annoyances filter

This raises serious concerns related to: > > * defamation > * privacy violations > * impersonation-style traffic manipulation > > While **b-short.link** may have legitimate uses, it is currently being widely used for deceptive redirection campaigns on social media platforms. > > ## Evidence (Search Results Showing Multiple Cases) > > Examples of related posts can be observed here: > > * [https://x.com/search?q=lang%3Aja%20b-short.link&src=typed_query&f=top](https://x.com/search?q=lang%3Aja%20b-short.link&src=typed_query&f=top) > * [https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.threads.com%2F+b-short.link](https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.threads.com%2F+b-short.link) > > ## Impact > > These posts appear frequently across X and Threads and may expose users to: > > * deceptive advertising > * unwanted subscriptions > * scam-style redirects > * privacy risks > > ## Request > > I hope you can investigate this activity and consider whether **b-short.link** should be reviewed for inclusion in filtering lists or abuse monitoring systems. > > Thank you for your time and for helping maintain a safer browsing environment. ### [NSFW] Screenshots [Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/i23vdxmd5a80k40owsgscw8o4w4kcgcqt2edlhpyqo.png?nc=1) ### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Windows v7.22.7 System version: | `Windows11,25H2` Browser: | Chrome AdGuard driver: | TDI Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
